2605.10934.title: Variational Inference for Lévy Process-Driven SDEs via Neural Tilting
2605.10934.paper_summary: Modelling extreme events and heavy-tailed phenomena is central to building reliable predictive systems in domains such as finance, climate science, and safety-critical AI. While Lévy processes provide a natural mathematical framework for capturing jumps and heavy tails, Bayesian inference for Lévy-driven stochastic differential equations (SDEs) remains intractable with existing methods: Monte Carlo approaches are rigorous but lack scalability, whereas neural variational inference methods are efficient but rely on Gaussian assumptions that fail to capture discontinuities. We address this tension by introducing a neural exponential tilting framework for variational inference in Lévy-driven SDEs. Our approach constructs a flexible variational family by exponentially reweighting the Lévy measure using neural networks. This parametrization preserves the jump structure of the underlying process while remaining computationally tractable. To enable efficient inference, we develop a quadratic neural parametrization that yields closed-form normalization of the tilted measure, a conditional Gaussian representation for stable processes that facilitates simulation, and symmetry-aware Monte Carlo estimators for scalable optimization. Empirically, we demonstrate that the method accurately captures jump dynamics and yields reliable posterior inference in regimes where Gaussian-based variational approaches fail, on both synthetic and real-world datasets.
2605.10934.paper_summary_zh: 建模極端事件和重尾現象對於在金融、氣候科學和安全關鍵的人工智慧等領域建立可靠的預測系統至關重要。雖然Lévy過程為捕捉跳躍和重尾提供了一個自然的數學框架，但對於Lévy驅動的隨機微分方程（SDEs）的貝葉斯推斷在現有方法中仍然難以處理：蒙特卡羅方法雖然嚴謹但缺乏可擴展性，而神經變分推斷方法效率高但依賴於高斯假設，無法捕捉不連續性。我們通過引入一個神經指數傾斜框架來解決這一矛盾，以進行Lévy驅動SDEs的變分推斷。我們的方法通過使用神經網絡對Lévy測度進行指數重加權來構建一個靈活的變分族。這種參數化保留了基礎過程的跳躍結構，同時仍然在計算上可處理。為了實現高效推斷，我們開發了一種二次神經參數化，該參數化產生了傾斜測度的閉合形式正規化、一種穩定過程的條件高斯表示，便於模擬，以及針對可擴展優化的考慮對稱的蒙特卡羅估計器。在實證中，我們展示了該方法能夠準確捕捉跳躍動態，並在高斯基礎的變分方法失效的情況下，對合成和實際數據集提供可靠的後驗推斷。
2605.10934.author: Yaman Kindap et.al.
2605.10934.authors: Yaman Kindap, Manfred Opper, Benjamin Dupuis, Umut Simsekli, Tolga Birdal
